options.blogdescription: A sample site for previewing WordPress themes.

steps.code:
<?php
require '/wordpress/wp-load.php';

// 1. Static front page. Look up the imported Home page by slug and set it
// as page_on_front so themes with a designed home pattern render that
// page instead of the latest-posts loop. show_on_front=page is required
// for both page_on_front and page_for_posts to take effect.
$home = get_page_by_path('home');
if ($home) {
    update_option('show_on_front', 'page');
    update_option('page_on_front', (int) $home->ID);
}

// 2. Classic-theme menu locations. The Customizer can't be driven by WXR
// alone; nav_menu_locations is a theme_mod that maps location slugs
// (registered by register_nav_menus()) to nav_menu term IDs. We pin our
// three imported menus to the conventional location slugs every classic
// theme uses (primary/footer/social), then pre-fill the same map for the
// most-used Twenty Twenty-* themes that diverge from the convention.
$locations = array();
foreach (array(
    'primary-menu' => array('primary', 'main', 'menu-1', 'header'),
    'footer-menu'  => array('footer', 'menu-2', 'footer-menu'),
    'social-menu'  => array('social', 'social-menu'),
) as $menu_slug => $location_slugs) {
    $term = get_term_by('slug', $menu_slug, 'nav_menu');
    if (! $term) { continue; }
    foreach ($location_slugs as $loc) {
        $locations[$loc] = (int) $term->term_id;
    }
}
if ($locations) { set_theme_mod('nav_menu_locations', $locations); }

// 3. Permalinks. Themes assume pretty permalinks; flush so /category/x/
// and /tag/x/ resolve immediately after import.
update_option('permalink_structure', '/%postname%/');
flush_rewrite_rules(false);
